consit.about: A world-famous pilgrimage center housing the sacred abode of Lord Murugan and the tourist hub of Kodaikanal. Despite its high revenue generation, it faces severe administrative and environmental neglect.

problem.title: Sanitation and River Pollution
problem.desc: The sacred Shanmuganadhi river is heavily polluted with sewage and waste dumping, while the town lacks a modern waste management system to handle the massive pilgrim footfall.

problem.title: District Bifurcation Demand
problem.desc: Residents face extreme hardship traveling 70km to the Dindigul Collectorate; there is a massive, unmet demand for Palani to be declared a separate district headquarters.

problem.title: Incomplete Infrastructure
problem.desc: Major projects like the Palani-Kodaikanal ropeway and the Palani-Erode railway line remain stuck in the proposal stage, hindering tourism and trade growth.

problem.title: Waterbody Siltation
problem.desc: The Vaiyapuri tank, a key water source and landmark, is heavily silted and requires urgent desilting to prevent summer water crises.

problem.title: Cold Storage for Ayakudi Guavas
problem.desc: Farmers of the famous Ayakudi guavas lack adequate government cold storage facilities, leading to post-harvest losses and price exploitation by middlemen.

consit.about: South India's primary vegetable supply hub. While it moves over 1,000 tonnes of produce daily, its infrastructure has become a major bottleneck for the agrarian economy.

problem.title: Market Design Failure
problem.desc: The new ₹21 crore Gandhi Market complex is a vertical failure; built on two levels without lifts, it is unusable for heavy vegetable sacks, forcing traders to stay in private lots.

problem.title: TASMAC Nuisance
problem.desc: The persistence of a government liquor shop (TASMAC) right in front of the market complex creates a hostile and unsafe environment for women laborers and farmers.

problem.title: Traffic Paralysis
problem.desc: The road connecting Palani Road to the market is too narrow for container trucks and buses, causing permanent gridlock during market hours.

problem.title: Lack of Integrated Processing
problem.desc: Despite the massive volume of vegetables, there is zero government investment in local food processing or value-addition units.

problem.title: Inadequate Public Parking
problem.desc: The absence of a designated heavy vehicle parking yard forces lorries to park on highways, leading to frequent accidents and congestion.

consit.about: A high-profile constituency often represented by senior ministers. It is a mix of traditional agriculture and expanding suburban residential zones.

problem.title: Water Supply Reliability
problem.desc: Despite promises of Vaigai river water, many villages like Pillaiyarnatham still suffer from erratic drinking water supply during the peak summer months.

problem.title: Stalled Industrial Growth
problem.desc: The announced TIDEL park and other industrial clusters are yet to see ground-level implementation, leading to high unemployment among local graduates.

problem.title: Reservoir Siltation
problem.desc: The Kamarajar reservoir and surrounding check-dams have lost significant storage capacity due to decades of silt accumulation.

problem.title: Encroached Common Lands
problem.desc: Illegal encroachments on water channels and grazing lands have reduced the natural groundwater recharge capacity of the constituency.

problem.title: Healthcare Access Gaps
problem.desc: While a new hospital was announced, existing rural health centers are understaffed and lack basic emergency trauma care facilities.

consit.about: A reserved constituency and a global hub for jasmine cultivation. The entire local economy is sensitive to the volatile flower market and agricultural water availability.

problem.title: Perfume Factory Demand
problem.desc: Jasmine farmers are forced to sell to private buyers at throwaway prices because the government has failed to establish a state-run perfume extraction unit.

problem.title: Secondary Healthcare Failure
problem.desc: The Nilakottai Government Hospital lacks specialist doctors, with most patients being referred to Madurai for even standard medical procedures.

problem.title: Cold Storage for Floriculture
problem.desc: Highly perishable flowers like roses and jasmine rot during market gluts due to the total absence of government-run cold storage.

problem.title: Groundwater Depletion
problem.desc: Unregulated borewell digging for flower farms has caused the water table to drop to dangerous levels, affecting rural drinking water wells.

problem.title: Middlemen Exploitation
problem.desc: Lack of a regulated market auction system for flowers allows middlemen to fix prices, leaving farmers with minimal profits.

consit.about: A hilly terrain famous for its premium mango and coconut crops. It remains one of the most industrially backward regions in the district.

problem.title: Mango Value-Chain Gaps
problem.desc: Premium Natham mangoes are sold raw to other districts; the absence of a local pulp factory or processing unit robs farmers of potential income.

problem.title: Wild Animal Crop Raids
problem.desc: Farmers on the foothills face constant destruction by wild boars and peacocks, with the forest department offering little to no compensation.

problem.title: Poor Interior Roads
problem.desc: Roads connecting hilly hamlets to the town are in deplorable condition, making it impossible for transport vehicles to reach farms during rains.

problem.title: Coconut Pest Infestation
problem.desc: The recurring invasive pest attacks on coconut groves are not being addressed with sufficient scientific or financial aid from the government.

problem.title: High Migration Rates
problem.desc: The total lack of local MSME units forces almost every educated youth to migrate to Chennai or Tiruppur for even low-wage work.

consit.about: The district headquarters and the historic 'City of Locks.' As of 2026, the town is struggling with 'Old City' infrastructure that cannot handle its new status as a Corporation.

problem.title: Lock Industry Extinction
problem.desc: The world-famous lock industry is dying due to zero modernization and lack of branding support, leaving thousands of heritage artisans in poverty.

problem.title: Bus Terminus Crisis
problem.desc: The Kamarajar bus stand is paralyzed by load; the proposed Pallapatti terminus is stalled due to environmental protests, leaving the city in transit chaos.

problem.title: Chronic Sewage Overflows
problem.desc: The underground drainage (UGD) system is incomplete and poorly designed, leading to raw sewage flooding the streets every time it rains.

problem.title: Gandhi Market Congestion
problem.desc: The central vegetable market remains an unplanned mess with no parking, causing permanent traffic gridlock in the heart of the town.

problem.title: Industrial Pollution
problem.desc: Effluent discharge from old tanneries continues to contaminate the local groundwater, making it unfit for consumption in several wards.

consit.about: A dry agrarian belt and a hub for small spinning mills. It is a region of high debt and low industrial diversity.

problem.title: Spinning Mill Closures
problem.desc: High power tariffs and cotton price fluctuations have led to the closure of dozens of small spinning mills, creating a local employment crisis.

problem.title: Tobacco Market Collapse
problem.desc: With the global shift away from tobacco, farmers have no government support or subsidy to transition to viable alternative crops.

problem.title: Shenbhagavalli Dam Ruin
problem.desc: The failure to reconstruct the Shenbhagavalli check-dam has deprived thousands of acres of irrigation, turning fertile lands into scrub.

problem.title: Drinking Water Scarcity
problem.desc: As a drought-prone belt, many villages still rely on overhead tanks that only receive water once every 10-15 days.

problem.title: Rural Debt Trap
problem.desc: The failure of both the textile and agriculture sectors has pushed a majority of rural families into the clutches of private moneylenders.
